sections.label: Lyrics
lines: In the hush before the morning breaks
In the shadows no one else could trace
I was still, unseen, unnamed
But You were there, and You knew my name
A whisper rose beneath the leaves
A breath that stirred eternity
Before the words had left my tongue
You said, "I saw you where you’d come from"
Under the fig tree, I was known
Not by men, but by the One alone
No mask, no shield, no veiling light
You saw my soul, and called it right
Under the fig tree, all was still
But You moved first, and You always will
Before the world had time to see
You had already chosen me
The sky was heavy with the unseen stars
And still You reached into my heart
No résumé, no grand acclaim
Yet still You whispered out my name
Where silence wept and roots ran deep
You spoke of heavens opened, free
A ladder stretched from earth to fire
You called me up — You called me higher
Under the fig tree, I was known
Not by sight, but by the One alone
Before the earth could spell my worth
You’d written love across my birth
Under the fig tree, still and small
You met me there — You knew it all
Before the curtain rose for me
You were already on your knees
No corner too quiet
No moment too soon
You saw through the stillness
And sang me in tune
You knew the map
Though I hadn’t moved
You saw the fig tree
And You saw through
Under the fig tree, I was known
Where heaven's eyes had made me home
Before the noise, before the crown
You lifted me without a sound
Under the fig tree, unashamed
No hiding left, no need for fame
Just You and I, and sacred ground
Where I was lost — and now I’m found
You saw me there… You see me still
